topic.name: Recreational Vessel Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts related to the regulation and management of recreational vessels in the United States.

Document 1: establish maximum passenger capacity and maximum weight capacity standards for recreational vessels
require manufacturers and operators of passenger vessels to permanently display maximum capacity requirements
Document 2: no permit shall be required for the discharge from a recreational vessel
defines a 'recreational vessel' as any vessel that is leased, rented, or chartered for pleasure
Document 5: define 'recreational vessel' as a vessel being manufactured or operated primarily for pleasure
requires the manufacturer or builder to bear the burden of establishing that a vessel is recreational
Document 4: require fishery management plans for overfished fisheries to specify a time period for ending overfishing
modifies the exceptions to the requirement that such period not exceed ten years

topic.name: Community Development and Environmental Sustainability
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives aimed at enhancing community welfare through environmental conservation, job training, and urban revitalization.

Document 1: facilitate the inventory of brownfield sites
return brownfields to productive use
Document 2: work experiences in public and nonprofit sector green-collar jobs
promote energy efficiency, energy conservation
Document 3: implement transformational programs in eligible neighborhoods
concentration of extreme poverty, severely distressed housing
Document 4: encourage establishment of comprehensive safety and health management systems
recognize achievement in protecting employees from occupational hazards
Document 7: encourage and test innovative vacant property reclamation
urban infrastructure renewal strategies in older industrial cities
Document 10: increase knowledge about conservation of the Great Lakes
provide education regarding the importance of conservation

topic.name: Offshore Energy Leasing Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation and management of offshore energy resources, including oil and gas leasing activities in coastal areas.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands located within 125 miles of the coast of New Jersey.
areas with respect to which a moratorium on oil and gas leasing activities existed before enactment of this Act.
Document 2: Coal Oversight and Leasing Reform Act of 2015 or the COAL Reform Act of 2015
repeal the requirement that the Department of the Interior offer under a deferred bonus lease payment system at least 50% of total acreage offered for coal leasing.
Document 3: direct the Department of Energy to require specified percentages of liquefied natural gas exports to be transported on vessels documented under U.S. law.
direct the President to require specified percentages of crude oil exports to be transported on vessels documented under U.S. law.
Document 4: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering any tract located in the following areas for either oil and gas leasing or preleasing
the Eastern Gulf of Mexico that is referred to in the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006.
Document 5: authorize the governor of Virginia to petition the Secretary of the Interior for authorization to conduct natural gas exploration and extraction activities
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit into a Clean Energy Fund of 50% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids.
Document 6: revise and increase the allocations with Gulf states of certain qualified outer Continental Shelf (OCS) revenues
Repeals the limitation placed upon the amount of distributed qualified OCS revenues.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act
Directs the Secretary to agree to a lessee's request to amend any lease issued for any Central and Western Gulf of Mexico tract.
Document 8: Allows the governor of a state to petition the Secretary of the Interior to make a new producing area of the state eligible for oil leasing
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit 45% of all rentals, royalties, bonus bids.
Document 9: Amends the Helium Act to require that all proceeds received by the Secretary of the Interior from the sale or disposition of helium on federal land be credited to the Helium Production Fund.
Directs the Secretary to undertake a national helium gas resource assessment.
Document 10: prohibit Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil or natural gas in any area of the outer Continental Shelf off the coast of California, Oregon, or Washington.

topic.name: Designation of Postal and VA Facilities
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures related to the naming and designation of various postal service and veterans affairs facilities across the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) at 9800 West Commercial Boulevard in Sunrise, Florida, as the 'William 'Bill' Kling VA Clinic.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 523 East Railroad Street in Knox, Pennsylvania, as the 'Specialist Ross A. McGinnis Memorial Post Office.'
Document 4: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Vet Center in Prescott, Arizona, as the 'Dr. Cameron McKinley Department of Veterans Affairs Veterans Center.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2886 Sandy Plains Road in Marietta, Georgia, as the 'Marine Lance Corporal Squire 'Skip' Wells Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1335 Jefferson Road in Rochester, New York, as the 'Specialist Theodore Matthew Glende Post Office.'
Document 10: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 13301 Smith Road in Cleveland, Ohio, as the 'David John Donafee Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative amendments aimed at reforming legal processes and enhancing oversight in the judicial system.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
excludes law firms from the definition of 'debt collector'
Document 2: amends the federal judicial code to narrow the scope of foreign sovereign immunity
authorizes U.S. courts to hear cases involving claims against a foreign state
Document 3: allows the return of personal property retained as evidence
in connection with an incident of sexual assault involving a member of the Armed Forces
Document 4: amends the Internal Revenue Service Restructuring and Reform Act
expand existing grounds for termination of IRS employees
Document 5: prohibits federal entities from using unmanned aircraft for surveillance
sets forth exceptions for the use of MAVDs
Document 6: requires DOJ consultation before prosecuting aliens for terrorist offenses
involves national security considerations
Document 7: authorizes state or local governments to modify federal consent decrees
places the burden of proof on the party filing the action
Document 8: prohibits performance awards for IRS employees with misconduct
protects the integrity of the IRS
Document 9: requires written justification from convening authority in court-martial actions
prohibits dismissal of guilty findings
Document 10: authorizes photographing and broadcasting of court proceedings
ensures due process rights are not violated

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Reforms
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at improving the Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on payment structures, access to services, and provider eligibility.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to exempt from the cap on payments for hospice care
qualifies a hospice program under this Act if at least 50% of the shares of its common stock are owned by a minority individual or group
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to waive coinsurance for colorectal cancer screening tests
thus covering 100% of their cost under Medicare part B
Document 3: Amends the Federal Employees' Compensation Act to include physician assistants, nurse practitioners, clinical nurse specialists
as eligible providers of medical, surgical, and hospital services
Document 4: amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to add standards for drug compendia
used by physicians with respect to payment for certain drugs
Document 5: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to revise Medicare payment for indirect medical education (IME) costs
in teaching hospitals
Document 6: authorizes qualified physical therapists to provide services for Medicare beneficiaries
without the requirement of a physician referral
Document 7: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to request the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) to develop standardized marketing requirements
for Medicare Advantage organizations
Document 8: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the outpatient therapy cap
to improve access to rehabilitation services
Document 9: directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to continue Medicaid spousal income and resource protections
for spouses of beneficiaries receiving home- and community-based services
Document 10: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act with respect to home health agencies
to revise surety bond condition of participation requirements

topic.name: Social Security and Economic Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at providing economic support and benefits related to Social Security, unemployment compensation, and assistance for seniors and veterans.

Document 1: Guaranteed 3% COLA for Seniors Act of 2011
require the use of such index to compute cost-of-living increases for Social Security benefits
Document 2: No Social Security for Illegal Immigrants Act of 2007
exclude from creditable wages and self-employment income any wages earned for services by aliens
Document 3: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014
extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals
Document 6: Safeguarding America's Seniors and Veterans Act of 2009
make a one-time payment of $500 to each eligible individual age 18 or over who was entitled to Social Security benefits
Document 8: Veterans Pensions Protection Act of 2016
exempts payments from annual income determinations with respect to pensions for veterans and surviving spouses

topic.name: Higher Education Act Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Higher Education Act of 1965 aimed at improving federal student loan programs and accountability in higher education institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend by one year the Secretary of Education's authority to purchase Federal Family Education Loans.
Extends by one year the authority of institutions of higher education to participate in the FFEL lender-of-last-resort program.
Document 2: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow borrowers of Direct Stafford Loans to opt to extend the repayment period.
Subsidizes the interest that accrues on Direct Stafford Loans for the first six months of that extended grace period.
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to replace several existing federal student loan programs with a single repayment plan.
Establishes the Income Dependent Education Assistance Loan Program and the IDEA Loan Repayment Program.
Document 4: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify reporting requirements for institutions of higher education.
Requires IHEs to submit additional student enrollment data to enable coding and reporting on students' level of academic preparation.
Document 5: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to make various entities eligible for funding under title IV.
Lists the items to be included in a state's alternative accreditation agreement with the Secretary.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to make available a list of accrediting agencies and institutions eligible under the Federal Family Education Loan program.
Conditions a school's eligibility for student assistance funding on providing notice of its recognition as a legitimate degree-granting institution.
Document 7: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify eligibility requirements for IHEs to participate in federal student aid programs.
Requires an IHE to provide a multi-year tuition and fee schedule to admitted students.
Document 8: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to permit a borrower to discharge student loans in bankruptcy.
Prohibits collecting the amount owed on a defaulted federal student loan through offsets of social security or tax refunds.
Document 9: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to eliminate the expected family contribution for certain students.
Requires financial aid administrators to verify eligibility for adjusted expected family contribution.
Document 10: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require IHEs to submit sufficient data for the Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System.
Directs the Secretary to establish common definitions for IHEs to follow in submitting data.

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating air transportation, enhancing safety, and improving airport infrastructure.

Document 1: prohibiting an air carrier from imposing any specified fees relating to a flight
establishing standards for assessing whether such fees are reasonable
Document 2: increased excise taxes on aviation fuels
extending authority for airport improvement project grants
Document 3: suspend for a specified period the excise tax on kerosene and certain liquids used as fuel in commercial aviation
Document 4: require the Secretary of Transportation to prohibit the operator of an unmanned recreational aircraft from flying it above 400 feet
where it may be seen from the airport's perimeter
Document 5: directs the Federal Aviation Administration to enter into a competitive cooperative agreement
establish minimum standards to ensure compliance with stage 4 noise levels
Document 6: encourages airport sponsors to take into account customer convenience and threats of a terrorist attack
adjust plans to address new terrorist threats
Document 7: prohibits the Secretary of Transportation from limiting commercial air traffic from one airport
unless consent is given by the Authority
Document 8: improve air service to underserved airports
requires priority to communities no longer receiving scheduled air service
Document 9: allows certain air carriers to use slots for nonstop air transportation service
between Reagan National Airport and another airport more than 1,250 statute miles away
Document 10: authorizes appropriations for civil aviation research and development
conduct research on improved runway surfaces and methods to improve certification of new technologies

topic.name: Public Safety and Justice Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public safety, crime reporting, and the welfare of vulnerable populations, particularly children and victims of violence.

Document 1: Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program Reauthorization Act of 2012
authorize appropriations for the Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant Program
Document 2: Illegal Alien Crime Reporting Act of 2009
compiles statistics on each person who is arrested, charged with a crime, convicted, or incarcerated after being convicted of a crime
Document 3: transfers the Office of Dispute Resolution in the Department of Justice
consolidating ineffective or duplicative programs
Document 4: Amends the Missing Children's Assistance Act
operation of a cyber tipline to provide online users and electronic service providers an effective means of reporting Internet-related child sexual exploitation
Document 5: Stop Child Abuse in Residential Programs for Teens Act of 2011
require each location of a covered program to meet specified minimum standards
Document 6: Department of Justice Global Advisory Committee Authorization Act of 2013
gather views from federal, state, local, and tribal agencies that work to support public safety and justice
Document 7: Campus Accountability and Safety Act
require institutions of higher education to include in their annual campus security reports
Document 8: Strengthening the Child Welfare Response to Human Trafficking Act of 2013
develop and publish guidelines to assist state, Indian tribe, and tribal organization child welfare agencies
Document 9: Amends the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006
repeal a provision ensuring governmental social service agencies with child protection responsibilities access to the national crime information databases
Document 10: Security and Financial Empowerment (SAFE) Act
sets forth entitlement standards and implementation guidelines for employee use of emergency leave to address domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, or stalking

topic.name: Clean Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative efforts to promote clean energy technologies, energy efficiency, and renewable energy sources.

Document 1: establish a Clean Energy Technology Manufacturing and Export Assistance Fund
promote policies that will reduce production costs and encourage innovation
Document 2: allows an energy tax credit for investment in waste heat to power property
generates electricity through the recovery of a qualified waste heat resource
Document 3: authorize projects for the purchase of electricity from renewable energy sources
establish a federal facility energy efficiency and renewable energy projects fund
Document 4: provides loans to states and Indian tribes to provide incentives for renewable energy sources
energy efficiency and energy conserving improvements
Document 5: direct the EPA Administrator to promulgate regulations requiring biodiesel in diesel fuel
Document 6: establish the Office of Energy Security to oversee federal energy security programs
provide a tax credit for production of qualified flexible fuel motor vehicles
Document 7: authorize the Secretary to sell from the Reserve in case of severe energy supply interruption
provide assistance to low-income consumers of heating oil
Document 8: provide grants to manufacturers who place energy efficient commercial building property in service
limits such a grant to the basis of such property
Document 9: extend the grant program for investment in renewable energy property
allow an energy tax credit for investment in qualified solar manufacturing project property
Document 10: increase the volume of renewable fuel required to be in gasoline
prescribe increasing minimum quantities of renewable fuel derived from cellulosic biomass

topic.name: Labor and Voting Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at protecting labor rights and ensuring fair voting processes in federal elections.

Document 1: Secret Ballot Protection Act of 2007
unfair labor practice for employers to bargain without a secret ballot election
Document 2: Offshore Worker Whistleblower Protection Act
prohibits retaliatory actions against employees reporting violations
Document 3: Lines Interfere with National Elections Act of 2012
requires states to provide adequate voting resources to reduce waiting times
Document 4: Offshore Oil and Gas Worker Whistleblower Protection Act of 2015
prohibits discrimination against employees reporting unsafe conditions
Document 5: Mail-In Ballot Tracking Act of 2007
requires states to implement procedures for tracking mail-in ballots
Document 6: Truth in Employment Act of 2013
amends labor practices regarding employment requirements
Document 7: Supplemental Mine Improvement and New Emergency Response Act of 2007
requires emergency response plans for miners
Document 8: Overseas Voting Practical Amendments Act of 2007
amends absentee voting processes for overseas voters
Document 9: Military and Overseas Voter Empowerment Act
establishes procedures for absentee voting for military and overseas voters
Document 10: Let My Absentee Ballot Count Act of 2010
requires tracking and confirmation procedures for absentee ballots

topic.name: Veteran and Elderly Health Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving healthcare access and services for veterans and the aging population in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include occupational therapists within the definition of 'behavioral and mental health professionals' for purposes of the National Health Service Corps.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a Geriatric and Gerontology Loan Repayment Program.
Requires the Secretary to establish a Health and Long-Term Care Workforce Advisory Panel to examine workforce issues related to health and long-term care for the aging population.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from collecting a copayment from a veteran being furnished inpatient or outpatient hospice care by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA).
Document 4: Allows a veteran enrolled in the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) annual patient enrollment system to elect to receive covered health services through a non-VA facility.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a program to screen veterans eligible for Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) hospital, medical, and nursing home care for symptoms of traumatic brain injury (TBI).
Document 6: Repeals the termination date of the requirement for an annual report from the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) on the VA's compliance with requirements for managing the provision of hospital care and medical services.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish a program to provide to veterans of Operations Iraqi Freedom and Enduring Freedom, particularly veterans who served in such Operations while in the National Guard and reserves: peer outreach and support services.
Document 8: Directs the Department of Health and Human Services to establish a demonstration program for states with a shortage of emergency medical technicians (EMTs) to assist veterans who completed military EMT training.
Document 9: Establishes in the Veterans Health Administration (VHA) of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA): the position of Director of Physical Therapy Service.
Document 10: Amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998 to require the Secretary of Labor to establish a health professions training demonstration project to train certain unemployed workers for employment as health care professionals.

topic.name: Energy Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: Legislation concerning the regulation, approval, and environmental assessment of energy infrastructure projects in the United States.

Document 1: Declares that a presidential permit shall not be required for the pipeline described in the application filed on May 4, 2012, by TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P.

Deems the final environmental impact statement issued by the Secretary of State on August 26, 2011, coupled with such Final Evaluation Report, to satisfy all requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Power Act and the Natural Gas Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to hold a public hearing in that locality, if requested by five or more individuals or an organization representing 30 or more individuals.
Requires the hearing to be held in a statistical metropolitan area at a location reasonably central to the affected areas, if a facility affects multiple areas.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Power Act to direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to designate one or more regions within the Eastern Interconnection and the Western Interconnection to be represented by specified regional transmission planners.

Requires the federal power marketing administrations and transmitting utilities in a designated region to integrate their transmission plans with the regional transmission plans required under this Act.
Document 4: Sets forth provisions to encourage the partial or complete remediation of inactive and abandoned mine sites for the public good by good samaritans.
Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to authorize a permitting authority to issue good samaritan discharge permits.
Document 5: This bill amends the Bald and Golden Eagle Protection Act to require the Department of the Interior to issue or deny an eagle permit for no less than 30 years.

This bill amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to exempt from criminal liability any taking, killing, or other harm to a migratory bird that is accidental or incidental to the presence or operation of an otherwise lawful activity.
Document 6: Authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC), upon the request of the licensee for the project numbered 12478-003 (Gibson Dam, Montana), to extend for six years the time period during which the licensee is required to commence construction.
Document 7: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to instruct the Secretary of Energy to implement, through cooperative agreements with private sector partners, programs to develop a standard design for each of two small modular reactors.
Requires the Secretary to select proposals for such cooperative agreements through the use of competitive procedures and an impartial review of their scientific and technical merit.
Document 10: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities for the import of crude oil and other hydrocarbons at the United States-Canada Border.

Declares that no permit pursuant to Executive Order 13337 or any other similar Executive Order regulating such activities at the U.S. border, and no additional environmental impact statement (EIS), shall be required for such Pipeline.

topic.name: Drug Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to the regulation, prosecution, and legal status of controlled substances and drug-related offenses.

Document 1: Transfers marijuana from schedule I to schedule II of the Controlled Substances Act (CSA).
Provides that in a state in which marijuana may be prescribed for medical use under state law, no provision of the CSA shall prohibit or restrict the prescription of marijuana for medical use.
Document 2: Requires the Attorney General's prior written approval for a federal prosecution of an offense under the Controlled Substances Act.
Modifies CSA to delete specified mandatory minimum terms of imprisonment.
Document 7: Provides for the retroactive application of the penalty amendments of the Fair Sentencing Act of 2010 to cocaine base offenses.
Authorizes a court to reduce the term of imprisonment consistent with the amendments made by such Act.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers' rights and interests in financial and service transactions.

1: prohibit a debt collector from bringing, or threatening to bring, legal action against any consumer on a debt in which the statute of limitations has expired.
Requires a debt collector... to disclose to the consumer that... the debt collector may not bring legal action against the consumer to collect the debt because the statute of limitations has expired.
3: excludes from a consumer report... information related to a veteran's choice medical program (program) debt;
A consumer reporting agency shall, within 30 days after receiving notice of such dispute, delete such information from the veteran's file and notify the furnisher and the veteran.
6: prescribe consumer protection guidelines for rental-purchase transactions
grants enforcement powers to the Federal Trade Commission and to state attorneys general.
7: makes a provision of a form contract void from the inception if it prohibits or restricts an individual from engaging in written, oral, or pictorial reviews
The standards under which provisions of a form contract are considered void under this Act shall not be construed to affect legal duties of confidentiality.
8: requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations that establish standards for the implementation of a mechanism by which individuals can indicate whether they prefer to have personal information collected
The rules must allow for the collection and use of personal information, notwithstanding the expressed preference of the individual.
9: limits electronic discovery (e-discovery) in any health care lawsuit against a covered entity relating to an EHR-related adverse event
Prohibits a claimant from commencing a lawsuit more than three years after the manifestation of injury or one year after the claimant discovers, or should have discovered, the injury.

topic.name: Tax Relief Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various acts that amend the Internal Revenue Code to provide tax relief and credits for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: extends the new markets tax credit through 2016
allows an offset against the alternative minimum tax (AMT) for such credit
Document 2: extend through 2014 the exclusion from gross income of income attributable to the discharge of indebtedness
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 3: allow caregivers of family members a refundable income-based tax credit
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 4: makes permanent the tax deductions for state and local sales taxes
exempts certain provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act
Document 5: allows certain small manufacturers a tax credit for their computer hardware and software expenses
limits such credit to the lesser of 50% of such expenses or $35,000
Document 6: allows a tax credit for 50 percent of the costs of reducing lead hazards
maximum credit of $3,000 for lead abatement costs
Document 7: reduces the rate of the excise tax on distilled spirits to $2.70 per proof gallon
amends the Internal Revenue Code
Document 8: makes permanent provisions of the Jobs and Growth Tax Relief Reconciliation Act
reduces tax rates on capital gain and dividend income
Document 9: establish new estate tax rates
allows a $1 million estate tax exclusion
Document 10: provides for the refundability of the child tax credit
renders certain limits on such credit inapplicable

topic.name: Water Management and Ecosystem Restoration
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to water management, ecosystem restoration, and resilience in various U.S. water systems and coastal areas.

Document 1: comprehensive study of the water management, needs, and conservation of the Apalachicola-Chattahoochee-Flint River System
recommendations for an approach to determine water limits that recognize the needs of all water users
Document 2: requires the Secretary concerned to treat a qualified local government... as a federal agency
analysis under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 of any water project undertaken by the Bureau or the Corps
Document 3: establish a national strategy to protect, maintain, and restore coastal and marine ecosystems
revise the strategy within five years and every ten years thereafter to reflect new information
Document 4: strengthen collaboration efforts among federal, state, local, and private participants
unify the efforts of participants to efficiently restore and protect the ecosystem
Document 5: increase the percentage of the aggregate amount made available for state drinking water treatment revolving loan funds
Document 6: conduct or commission studies to strengthen the implementation of the Comprehensive Conservation and Management Plan for the Long Island Sound
develop and implement strategies to increase public education and awareness
Document 7: conduct a study to identify the risks and vulnerabilities of the coastal areas
develop a long-term strategy for addressing such increased damage
Document 8: extend through FY2017 the authorization of appropriations for allocations to carry out approved wetlands conservation projects
Document 9: establish financial reporting requirements for restoration activities in the Chesapeake Bay watershed
provide recommendations to the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency
Document 10: require the Federal Emergency Management Agency to establish a program to provide grant assistance to states for use in rehabilitating publicly-owned dams
develop a risk-based priority system for identifying deficient dams

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Customs Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating trade practices, enforcing customs laws, and addressing issues related to international trade and economic relations.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the Commissioner responsible for U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to initiate, upon petition or a referral from another federal agency, an investigation into claims of evasion of antidumping or countervailing duties.
Requires the Commissioner to suspend liquidation of each unliquidated entry of the covered merchandise and extend the liquidation of covered merchandise entered before such determination.
Document 2: Requires the President to impose sanctions pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act against a person that continues to knowingly and directly provide specialized financial messaging services to North Korea.
Imposes restrictions on financial institutions that facilitate transactions for nuclear development on North Korea's behalf.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that cyber economic espionage should be a priority issue in all economic and diplomatic discussions.
Directs the President to submit to Congress a list of foreign government officials responsible for cyber espionage of intellectual property of U.S. persons.
Document 4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to direct the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to distribute semiannually all funds from duties assessed pursuant to a countervailing duty order or antidumping duty order.
Defines terms related to affected domestic producers and eligible employees in the context of antidumping and countervailing duties.
Document 5: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to increase from $200 to $1,000 the value of articles that may be imported duty-free into the United States.
Addresses customs regulations regarding the importation of goods.
Document 6: Prohibits the importation of gum Arabic from Sudan.
Amends the Tariff Suspension and Trade Act of 2000.
Document 7: States the policy of the United States regarding advocacy for fair treatment of persons in whose favor judgments have been awarded by U.S. courts.
Imposes restrictions on judgment evading foreign states and their access to U.S. capital markets.
Document 8: Expresses the sense of Congress that the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) should enforce U.S. customs laws with respect to imports of textile or apparel articles.
Requires the seizure and forfeiture of imported textile or apparel articles for which a trade preference has been claimed.
Document 9: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to revise the prohibition against the President's designation of a beneficiary developing country for purposes of duty-free treatment.
Prohibits designation if a country enters into agreements that negatively affect trade with the U.S.
Document 10: Authorizes the President to give preferential treatment to certain articles imported from Nepal if specific requirements are met.
Grants duty-free treatment to certain textile or apparel articles imported directly from Nepal.

topic.name: Smart Manufacturing and Technology Innovation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at advancing smart manufacturing technologies, enhancing cybersecurity, and promoting innovation in various technology sectors to improve U.S. competitiveness and efficiency.

Document 1: requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to complete a national plan for smart manufacturing technology development
expand the scope of technologies covered by Industrial Assessment Centers to include smart manufacturing technologies
Document 2: directs specified federal agencies to transmit a cybersecurity strategic research and development plan
expand permitted National Science Foundation (NSF) grants for basic research on innovative approaches to enhance computer security
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Education to award grants to establish or expand STEM secondary schools
requires the NSF Director to administer a Technology Commercialization Awards Pilot Program
Document 4: authorizes the Department of Homeland Security to establish a joint United States-Israel Cybersecurity Center of Excellence
promote joint research and development, share best practices and access to American and Israeli innovation
Document 5: requires the Director of NIST to establish an initiative for advancing health care information enterprise integration
supporting the establishment of conformance testing infrastructure
Document 6: requires the National Science Foundation to establish a Global Warming Education Program to apply the latest scientific and technological discoveries
develop climate science K-12 curriculum and supplementary educational materials
Document 7: requires DOE to conduct a program of research, development, engineering, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes
support R&D activities and maintain competency in mid- to long-term transformational vehicle technologies
Document 8: requires the NSF to award competitive grants for basic research on innovative approaches to improve health care information systems
establish multidisciplinary Centers for Informatics Research
Document 9: directs DOE to establish and carry out a smart energy and water efficiency management pilot program
demonstrate advanced and innovative technology-based solutions that will increase and improve energy efficiency
Document 10: directs the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Energy to collaborate to improve assessments of energy critical elements
establish a research program to advance basic knowledge and enable expanded availability of designated energy critical elements

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Assistance and Diplomacy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing U.S. foreign relations through assistance, educational exchanges, and development initiatives.

1: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to credit to the International Litigation Fund proceedings- or claims-related funds received by the Department of State.
Transfers statutory responsibility for performing actuarial duties related to the Department's retirement systems from the Secretary of the Treasury to the Secretary of State.
2: Amends the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956 to provide assistance to combat HIV/AIDS in India.
3: Authorizes the Department of State to carry out, in cooperation with the government of India, the Gandhi-King Scholarly Exchange Initiative comprised of educational, scholarly, and professional exchange programs.
4: Authorizes the President, after congressional consultation, to designate one private, nonprofit organization as the American Enterprise Fund for Haiti to receive funds and support under this Act to promote Haitian private sector development.
6: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to state that it is U.S. policy to work with foreign countries and international and civil society organizations to increase the global commitment to achieving universal basic education.
7: Directs the Secretary of State to establish and administer a Global Service Fellowship Program to fund fellowships to promote international volunteering opportunities.
9: Expresses the sense of Congress that the protection of the rights of women and girls in Afghanistan and their full participation in the reestablishment of democracy are essential to Afghanistan's reconstruction.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at supporting agricultural producers through financial assistance, crop insurance reforms, and conservation programs.

Document 1: agricultural programs under the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
suspends permanent price support authorities
Document 2: prohibit the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation from paying a premium subsidy
crop insurance policy that is based on the actual market price
Document 3: supplemental direct commodity payment to eligible producers
provide supplemental assistance to eligible producers and first-handlers
Document 4: make grants to states and tribal governments to encourage owners and operators
initiate or expand maple-tapping activities
Document 5: extend conservation reserve and related programs
assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers
Document 6: encourage owners and operators of privately-held farm, ranch, and forest land to voluntarily make such land available
public access for wildlife-dependent recreation
Document 7: extend program funding through FY2018
provide loan guarantees and grants to agricultural producers
Document 8: repeals and reforms direct payments, countercyclical payments
establishes the agriculture risk coverage program
Document 9: requires each federal milk marketing order to establish a referendum information clearinghouse
provide producers with referendum alerts
Document 10: give the operator, tenant, or sharecropper on a farm an opportunity to make an annual election
authorize the Commodity Credit Corporation to offer supplemental coverage

topic.name: Federal Recognition and Land Trusts
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts that extend federal recognition to Indian tribes and address land trust issues for those tribes.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Reorganization Act to make it applicable to all federally recognized Indian tribes.
overrules the Supreme Court's decision in Carcieri v. Salazar.
Document 2: Amends the Siletz Tribe Indian Restoration Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust for the Siletz Tribe additional lands.
Prohibits gaming on lands taken into trust pursuant to this Act.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Directs the Secretary to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land.
Document 4: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida.
Requires that the membership roll of the Nation be determined in accordance with the membership criteria established.
Document 5: Provides that if the Cocopah Indian Tribe of Arizona transfers title to certain described land to the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary shall take it into trust for the benefit of the Tribe.
Designates Yuma county, Arizona, to be the Tribe's service area for the delivery of federal services.
Document 6: Amends federal law relating to the Lumbee Indians of North Carolina to extend federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina.
Document 8: Permits land held in trust for the Kalispel Tribe of Indians and the Puyallup Tribe of Indians to be leased for a term of greater than 25 years.
includes the Puyallup Tribe of Indians among those tribes permitted to lease restricted lands.
Document 9: Amends the Departments of Veterans Affairs and Housing and Urban Development to consider members of the Pascua Yaqui Tribe as residing on an Indian reservation.
for purposes of the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: This cluster discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and supporting democratic reforms and counterterrorism efforts in various countries.

1: Prohibits the head of an executive agency from entering into a contract with Rosoboronexport
Imposes procurement and export sanctions on any U.S. person that transfers goods or technology
2: It is the policy of the United States to assist the government of Tunisia in eliminating terrorist organizations
The bill authorizes the Department of State to provide assistance to strengthen and reform Tunisia's security sector
3: States that, in order to enhance the security of the Western Hemisphere, it shall be U.S. policy to promote the signing of counter terrorism conventions
Authorizes the President to impose specified sanctions on any country in the Western Hemisphere engaged in state sponsored terrorism
5: Directs the President to impose U.S.-based property blocking and entry sanctions against any foreign person that threatens the peace, security, or stability of Iraq or Syria
The President may furnish assistance to support humanitarian activities inside and outside of Syria
7: Requires that all U.S.-imposed sanctions with respect to Belarus remain in effect until the President certifies to Congress that the government of Belarus has met specified conditions
The President shall include the Joint Stock Company Belaruskali on the list of specially designated nationals
8: States that it is U.S. policy to support the democratic aspirations of Burma's people
Subjects persons identified to U.S. entry prohibition and financial sanctions
9: The Secretary of State is authorized to revoke the passport of any U.S. citizen whose activities abroad are likely to seriously damage U.S. national security
Should use such authority to revoke the passports of U.S. citizens providing material support to ISIS

topic.name: Regulatory Reform and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the regulatory process, enhancing transparency, and reducing burdens on businesses and agencies.

Document 1: establishes the Indian Education Agency to replace the Bureau of Indian Education
must prioritize schools with the most serious deficiencies in capacity or facility conditions.
Document 2: provide for a public notice and comment period before issuing the guidance in final form
make available to the public, when issuing guidance in final form, all studies, data, and other analysis relied upon by the CFPB.
Document 3: requires agencies, in making a rule, to base all preliminary and final determinations on evidence
requires the publication of advance notice of proposed rulemaking for major rules.
Document 4: prohibits covered regulations from being in effect during the time-out period
defines a 'covered regulation' as a final regulation that did not take effect before September 1, 2011.
Document 5: prohibits the Chief of the Army Corps of Engineers from taking any action to establish a restricted area prohibiting public access
requires the Chief to cease implementing any restricted area resulting from such action.
Document 6: establishes the Retrospective Regulatory Review Commission to conduct a review of the Code of Federal Regulations
goal is to achieve a reduction of at least 15% in the cumulative costs of regulation.
Document 7: requires initial and final regulatory flexibility analyses under RFA
includes tribal organizations within the definition of 'small governmental jurisdictions'.
Document 8: requires a federal agency that publishes a final major rule to publish, biannually while the rule remains in effect, a report on the benefits and costs
assess the effectiveness and benefits of the rule in producing its regulatory objectives.
Document 9: requires the EPA to include an offset in any proposed rule that limits greenhouse gas emissions
prohibits agencies from passing on the cost imposed by the rule on to any rate payer.
Document 10: requires federal agencies to review their covered rules and modify, consolidate, or terminate such rules
provides for judicial review of a denial or ...

topic.name: Tax Administration and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at reforming tax administration, simplifying tax filing processes, and enhancing taxpayer protections in the United States.

Document 1: IRS should offer pre-prepared tax returns by 2018.
Requires reporting on barriers to offering pre-prepared tax returns.
Document 2: Repeals income tax and imposes a national sales tax.
Grants states authority for sales tax collection.
Document 3: Grants Treasury authority to regulate tax return preparers.
Imposes minimum competency standards for tax preparers.
Document 4: Amends tax administration provisions for taxpayer protections.
Requires IRS to notify taxpayers of unauthorized identity use.
Document 5: Makes permanent the election to deduct state and local sales taxes.
Document 6: Prohibits state income tax on nonresident telecommuters.
Document 7: Allows taxpayers to designate payments to the Treasury.
Document 8: Amends penalties for erroneous tax returns with safe harbor rule.
Document 9: Allows tax deduction for state and local sales taxes.
Document 10: Directs IRS to implement Free File program for low-income taxpayers.

topic.name: Federal Budget Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and transparency in federal budgeting and appropriations processes.

Document 1: prohibit the receipts and disbursements of the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund from being included in the federal budget baseline
prohibits their availability for the establishment or funding of private accounts.
Document 2: repeal provisions establishing and appropriating funds to the Prevention and Public Health Fund
rescinds any unobligated balances appropriated to such Fund.
Document 3: subject the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to regular appropriations
repeals the requirement of an annual transfer to the CFPB of funds from the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.
Document 4: requires each federal department and agency to report to the congressional appropriations committees an operating budget
reflects a 5% reduction from the amount requested for it in the President's most recent budget submission.
Document 5: prohibits the use of funds appropriated under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act for physical signage
requires the head of each affected agency to report to the Office of Management and Budget a determination of such amount.
Document 10: requires the Secretary of the Treasury to include in the audited financial statement a calculation of the net present value of the overall fiscal exposures of the U.S. government
directs the President to take this financial statement into consideration in preparing the federal budget.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at reforming health insurance coverage, access to care, and related provisions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for qualified health insurance costs
Provides for the establishment and governance of individual membership associations (IMAs) to make available health benefits coverage
Document 2: Provides states with three options regarding title I of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
Amends title XIX of the Social Security Act to disregard assets in an HSA for purposes of determining Medicaid eligibility
Document 3: Permits a health insurance issuer that is licensed to offer child-only health insurance coverage in one state to also offer such coverage to eligible residents of other states
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for the application of a uniform annual enrollment period
Document 4: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act that require individuals to maintain minimum essential health care coverage
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal provisions added by the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act requiring certain employers to provide health insurance coverage
Document 6: Amends part A of title IV of the Social Security Act to make appropriations to expand access to high-quality child care for low-income families
Document 7: Amends the Public Health Service Act to maintain the requirement that health insurers provide coverage to dependents over 18 years of age until the dependent turns 26
Document 8: Requires child care providers to obtain a comprehensive criminal background check of each employee who provides child care services
Document 9: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to permit a health plan to decline coverage of specific items and services that are contrary to the religious beliefs
Document 10: Requires the federal government to ensure coverage for abortion care in public health insurance programs
Prohibits state and local governments from restricting insurance coverage of abortion care

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving various aspects of public health, including patient-centered care, pandemic preparedness, opioid abuse prevention, and children's health.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convene a Patient-Centered Health Care and Quality of Life Stakeholder Strategic Summit
to improve patient-centered care and address quality of life concerns for individuals with serious, complex, and chronic illness.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to submit to the Director of the World Health Organization (WHO) a proposal related to establishing a Pandemic Fund
Requires the Secretary to strengthen, expand, and coordinate domestic pandemic influenza preparedness activities.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to convene an interagency task force to develop, solicit input on, disseminate information about, and study implementation of best practices for pain management
Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize the Department of Justice to award grants to implement comprehensive community-wide prevention strategies.
Document 4: Requires states, as a condition of receiving funds under any program or activity administered by the Secretary of Education, to develop and implement policies and procedures prohibiting school personnel from requiring a child to obtain a prescription for any specified controlled substances
Directs the Comptroller General to review and report on prescription rates of medications used in public schools to treat children diagnosed with attention deficit disorder.
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman
Subjects an abortion provider who knowingly or recklessly fails to comply with this Act to civil penalties.
Document 6: Requires a group health plan that covers surgical benefits to also cover outpatient and inpatient diagnosis and treatment of a child's congenital or developmental deformity
Defines 'treatment' to include reconstructive surgical procedures that are performed on abnormal structures of the body.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide for the planning and implementation of a national public outreach and educational campaign to raise public awareness of women's preventive health
Establish a national media campaign and maintain and update an appropriate Internet website as part of such campaign.
Document 8: Amends the Public Health Service Act to include among the reasons preferences shall be given in awarding mosquito control program grants
Reauthorizes appropriations for FY2009-FY2013 for mosquito control programs to prevent and control mosquito-borne diseases.
Document 9: Directs the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to reduce the administrative burdens of researchers funded by the NIH.
Document 10: Provides for a tax exclusion of compensation paid to a member of the Armed Forces serving in Operation United Assistance in the Ebola virus disease outbreak area
Requires that such service member undergo a program of at least 21 days of controlled monitoring upon return from the outbreak area.

topic.name: Federal Government Oversight and Improvement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing oversight, accountability, and service delivery within various federal government agencies.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish a publicly available website for tracking, in detail, all expenditures of funds over $500 which are appropriated or otherwise available to the federal government.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to develop government-wide standards for customer service delivery, which shall be included in the Federal Government Performance Plan.
Directs the Performance Improvement Officer for each executive agency to establish customer service standards in accordance with such government-wide standards.

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures related to U.S. national security, military operations, and the treatment of detainees.

Document 1: Rewards for Justice program authorized under the State Department Basic Authorities Act of 1956

information leading to the arrest or conviction in any country of any individual for committing, conspiring or attempting to commit, or aiding or abetting in the commission of the kidnapping and murder of James Foley or Steven Sotloff.
Document 2: Civil Liberties Act of 2012
disposition under the law of war shall occur immediately upon custody and shall only mean transfer for trial and proceedings by a court established under article III of the Constitution.
Document 4: Preserving Freedom from Unwarranted Surveillance Act of 2012

Prohibits a person or entity acting under the authority of (or funded in whole or in part by) the federal government from using a drone to gather evidence or other information pertaining to criminal conduct.
Document 5: Directs the President to transition the mission of U.S. forces in Iraq
conducting limited scope and duration operations against al Qaeda and other international terrorist organizations.
Document 10: Prohibits the transfer to, or release in, the United States of any unprivileged enemy belligerent
includes any individual (other than a member of the Armed Forces) under detention at United States Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating transportation infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to award grants to eligible applicants to purchase or rehabilitate domestically manufactured rolling stock for intercity passenger rail transportation.
Directs Amtrak to establish a Next Generation Corridor Equipment Pool Committee.
Document 2: Revises the definition of 'commercial motor vehicle' with respect to federal commercial motor vehicle safety laws.
Prohibits the Secretary of Transportation from withholding commercial motor vehicle safety improvement grants from a state.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to require the person purchasing or operating a gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facility to inspect it.
Applies this inspection requirement with respect to newly listed abandoned pipelines.
Document 4: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Act to extend eligibility for projects of national and regional significance.
Defines 'aerotropolis transportation system' as a planned and coordinated multimodal freight and passenger transportation network.
Document 5: Revises urbanized area formula grant recipient requirements to ensure a fare of no more than 50% of the peak hour fare for U.S. veterans.
Transportation using or involving a facility or equipment of a project financed by the grant.
Document 6: This bill revises the railroad safety risk reduction program.
The bill extends the deadline for submission to the DOT by each Class I railroad carrier for implementing a positive train control (PTC) system.
Document 7: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking.
Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines.
Document 8: This bill makes a bikeshare project eligible to be an associated transit improvement project.
Public transportation capital projects shall include any project for the acquisition or replacement of bicycles and related equipment.
Document 9: Authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to make emergency grants to states and designated recipients for the operating costs of public transportation projects.
Sets the amount of a grant, at the option of the recipient, at up to 100% of the net cost of a project.
Document 10: Prohibits any transportation by rail of crude oil with a Reid vapor pressure level greater than 8.5 psi until the Department of Transportation establishes an appropriate national standard.
DOT shall establish such a national standard, after which the 8.5 psi standard required by this Act shall expire.

topic.name: Water Rights and Infrastructure
topic.description: Legislation and agreements focused on the development, management, and rights associated with water resources and infrastructure, particularly for tribal and regional needs.

Document 1: Amends the Mni Wiconi Project Act of 1988 to direct the Secretary of the Interior to develop plans for completing water supply systems for tribes.
Requires the Secretary to enter into agreements with multiple federal agencies to fulfill water supply and public health needs.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to develop inspection guidelines for project facilities that could pose risks to public safety.
Requires the Secretary to provide recommendations for improvement of operation and maintenance processes.
Document 3: Authorizes the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement entered into by the Hualapai Tribe and other entities.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to execute the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Agreement.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to provide technical assistance and grants for the development of comprehensive state water plans in New Mexico.
Conducts a comprehensive study of groundwater resources in New Mexico to assess quantity and quality.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to enter into a cooperative agreement for the planning, design, and construction of a regional water authority system.
Limits the federal share of planning, design, and construction of the System to 75% of the total cost.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to plan, design, and construct a regional water system in accordance with a settlement agreement among multiple parties.
Authorizes New Mexico and Santa Fe County to modify the extent, size, and capacity of the County Distribution System.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to participate in planning and construction of water recycling facilities in Arizona.
Limits the federal share of the project's cost to 25%.
Document 9: Ratifies the water rights compact between the Crow Tribe and the state of Montana.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to rehabilitate and improve the water diversion features of the Crow Irrigation Project.
Document 10: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to enter into cooperative agreements for the planning, design, and construction of water reclamation and reuse facilities in California.
Limits the federal share to 25% of the total cost of the first phase of the project.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating environmental safety standards, including water quality, greenhouse gas emissions, and public health protections.

1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to identify and establish standards for contaminants in bottled water.
Requires the Secretary to make certain additional information available to the public about the regulation of contaminants in bottled drinking water.
2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require covered persons to purchase a federal emission permit for the sale, combustion, or other use of greenhouse gas emission substances.
Establishes emission reduction targets for 2015 through 2059.
6: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to require the Environmental Protection Agency to revise the national primary drinking water regulation by lowering the action level for lead in drinking water.
The current action level for lead is 15 parts per billion.
10: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to revise and expand requirements for assessment by covered water systems of their vulnerability to a range of intentional acts.

Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to promulgate regulations to establish increasingly stringent, risk-based performance standards for the security of such systems.

topic.name: Legal Protections for Equality
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative efforts aimed at promoting equality and protecting against discrimination based on sexual orientation, gender identity, and religious beliefs.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to remove professional football leagues from the list of tax-exempt organizations.
Document 2: Amends federal copyright law to revise the definition of 'widow' or 'widower' for purposes of provisions concerning the transfer of a copyright to an author's spouse.
Document 3: Prohibits discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity in housing sales and rentals.
Document 4: Prohibits discrimination for or against federal employees on the basis of sexual orientation.
Document 5: Prohibits the term 'marriage' from including any relationship that the state does not recognize as a marriage.
Document 6: Requires employers to make an affirmative effort to reasonably accommodate the religious practices of employees.
Document 7: Prohibits personnel actions from being made on the basis of sexual orientation or gender identity.
Document 8: Prohibits the IRS from asking taxpayers about religious, political, or social beliefs.
Document 9: Requires accommodation of religious beliefs concerning human sexuality for members of the Armed Forces.
Document 10: Makes the Religious Freedom Restoration Act inapplicable to federal laws that protect against discrimination.

topic.name: Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures related to immigration, asylum, and national security, focusing on the regulation of foreign nationals and their status in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Passport Act of 1926 to authorize the Department of State to refuse to issue a passport to an individual who has aided, assisted or otherwise helped a foreign terrorist organization.
Authorizes the Department to revoke a passport previously issued to any such individual.
Document 3: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Department of Homeland Security to authorize employment for an asylum applicant who is not in detention.
Employment authorization shall be: (1) for a period of one year, and (2) renewable for additional one-year periods for the entire period necessary to adjudicate the applicant's asylum claim.
Document 5: This bill shifts from the Attorney General to the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) the authority to parole an alien temporarily into the United States, on a case-by-case basis.
A public interest parole is limited to instances where the alien has assisted the U.S. government in a matter, such as a criminal investigation, espionage, or other similar law enforcement activity.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing benefits, healthcare, and financial support for military veterans and their families.

Document 1: lifetime National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass available, at a cost of $10, to veterans
honoring service through access to national parks
Document 2: eliminates the requirement for medical and dental care eligibility based on age
members of the Armed Forces entitled to retired pay for non-regular service
Document 3: reduces minimum age for military retired pay from 60 to 55
benefits for non-regular service members
Document 4: prioritizes pay and allowances for members of the Armed Forces
ensures payment during periods of lapsed appropriations
Document 5: removes requirement for active duty days to reduce retirement age
benefits for reserve service members
Document 6: provides uncharged leave or monetary benefit to former members of the Armed Forces
administrative absence program
Document 7: repeals provisions requiring offset of dependency compensation
restores annuity eligibility for surviving spouses
Document 8: automatically enrolls certain veterans in the VA medical system
option for declining enrollment
Document 9: requires review of bonuses received by Army National Guard members
financial relief for erroneous payments
Document 10: ensures continued enrollment in TRICARE Prime for beneficiaries
allows election to switch to TRICARE Standard

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs
topic.description: The documents discuss the temporary suspension of duties on various products within the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain light absorbing photo dyes.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on modified phenolic resin in alkaline solution.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extends the temporary suspension of duty on butanedioic acid, dimethyl ester.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's non-work footwear.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: extends the temporary suspension of duty on dry adhesive copolyamide pellets.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on floor coverings and mats, of over 30% recycled Polyethylene (PE) or Ethylene-Vinyl Acetate (EVA).
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 7: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 8: suspend temporarily the duty on manganese ferrite carrier covered with acrylic resin.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 9: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of specific chemical compounds in propylene glycol monomethyl ether.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 10: suspend temporarily the duty on certain children's textile upper footwear.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization and Appropriations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures authorizing budget allocations and personnel for U.S. intelligence and defense agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various agencies.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2012, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: This bill authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
The amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2017, for such activities are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 3: Amends the National Security Agency Act of 1959 and the Inspector General Act of 1978 to require the Director of the National Security Agency (NSA) to be appointed by the President.
Document 4: This bill makes technical amendments to statutory references to title 50 (War and National Defense) of the U.S. Code, especially with respect to national security and miscellaneous intelligence community authorities.
Document 8: Requires funds appropriated for an intelligence or intelligence-related activity that are in excess of the amount specified for such activity in the funding tables to be specifically authorized by Congress.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating telecommunications services, ensuring consumer protection, and facilitating access to communication technologies.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to declare that a provider of voice services has the duty to provide number portability
Requires designation of 9-1-1 as the universal emergency telephone number within the United States
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the FCC to consider regulation of inmate telephone service
Prescribing a maximum uniform per-minute rate
Document 3: Amends federal copyright law and the Communications Act of 1934 to allow satellite or cable retransmission of signals
Allows retransmission of broadcast television stations located in the capital of a state
Document 4: Permits a satellite carrier to retransmit the signals of a state-wide network of noncommercial educational broadcast stations
Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to permit retransmission outside the local market
Document 5: Amends the Public Telecommunications Act of 1992 to require regulations prohibiting indecent programming
Maintains a policy that a single word or image may constitute indecent programming
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require a telecommunication carrier to provide call location information
Prohibits any cause of action against providers for providing such information in good faith
Document 7: Directs the FCC to issue rules to ensure providers of mobile broadband Internet access service offer mobile devices capable of receiving WiFi
The Lifeline program provides discounts on telephone service to qualifying low-income consumers
Document 8: Prohibits a state or local jurisdiction from imposing multiple or discriminatory taxes on digital goods or services
Excludes telecommunications service from the definition of digital service
Document 9: Prohibits states from imposing any new discriminatory tax on mobile services for three years
Defines 'new discriminatory tax' as a tax not generally imposed on other types of services
Document 10: Requires disclosure of service terms and conditions in marketing materials for advanced wireless mobile broadband service
Directs the FCC to set minimum signal strength and data rates for coverage area

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in campaign financing and lobbying activities in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit the use of any contribution made to a candidate for election to federal office
payment of a salary to the candidate or individual
Document 2: require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate
Document 4: a $200 tax credit for contributions to a candidate for federal elective public office
a tax deduction for such political contributions up to $600
Document 5: an outright ban at any time on such contacts
specifies a one-year ban on lobbying contacts by former elected officers
Document 10: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 with respect to lobbying disclosures reports
requires public disclosure by Members of Congress of employment negotiations

topic.name: Education Reform and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational outcomes and support systems for at-risk students in K-12 education.

Document 1: Establishes a transition-to-success mentoring program
assist at-risk students in transitioning from middle to high school.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to allocate grants to states
to modernize, renovate, or repair early learning or elementary or secondary education facilities.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award matching six-year grants
to establish or support dual enrollment programs.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants
to improve physical fitness education and curricula in elementary and secondary schools.
Document 9: Amends the School Dropout Prevention program
to create a research-based Statewide Graduation Success Plan addressing the factors associated with the risk of not graduating from high school.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Education to make grants to states
to implement state middle grades needs analyses and improvement plans.

topic.name: National Forest and Wilderness Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation, management, and conservation of national forests and wilderness areas in the United States.

1: Declares that the fire hazard and human endangerment in national forests to be designated by the Secretary of Agriculture constitute emergency circumstances.
Makes provisions of the Healthy Forests Restoration Act regarding hazardous fuel reduction on federal land applicable to all Forest Service projects.
2: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness.
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness.
3: Designates the federal land within the Monongahela National Forest in West Virginia as a wilderness area.
Inclusion in the National Wilderness Preservation System.
4: Establishes the Berryessa Snow Mountain National Conservation Area for the conservation and protection of ecological and recreational resources.
Requires a comprehensive plan for the protection and management of the federal lands included within the Conservation Area.
