9: Prohibits the Department of Agriculture and the Department of the Interior from adopting regulations that would delay emergency response personnel from entering wilderness areas.
Any entrance by emergency response personnel into a wilderness area in response to an emergency shall be reported to the appropriate Department.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions, ensuring consumer protection, and enhancing transparency in the financial sector.

Document 1: Repeals the subpoena and enforcement powers of the Director of the Federal Insurance Office
Prescribes the treatment of data collected by financial regulators from a nonbank financial company that is an insurance company
Document 2: Amends the Federal Reserve Act
Declares a borrower ineligible to borrow from any emergency lending program unless certified by federal banking regulators
Document 3: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956
Requires financial institutions to meet the needs of low- and moderate-income neighborhoods
Document 4: Authorizes covered financial institutions to conduct a contest, known as a 'savings promotion raffle'
Subjects such a drawing contest to regulations promulgated by the appropriate prudential regulator
Document 5: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to require the investment of annual surplus in marketable interest-bearing obligations
Prohibits disinvestment of Social Security Trust Fund amounts from public debt obligations
Document 6: Imposes an excise tax on over-the-counter derivative transactions
Defines 'derivative financial instrument' as any option, forward contract, or similar financial instrument
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act with respect to the presumption of a mortgagor's ability to pay
Increases the size of certain small insured depository institutions eligible for an 18-month on-site examination cycle
Document 8: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to rely upon examinations conducted by a state supervisory agency
Instructs the Secretary to consult with state supervisory agencies when issuing rules
Document 9: Prohibits financial holding companies from engaging in real estate brokerage or management activities
Exempts activities directly related to managing any real property owned by national banks
Document 10: Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal the prohibition against the payment of interest on demand deposits

topic.name: Homeland Security and Postal Services
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislation related to the security and operational efficiency of postal services and transportation security in the United States.

Document 1: Secure Delivery for America Act of 2015
provide access to secure, convenient package delivery receptacles
Document 2: transfer annually, without further appropriation, unclaimed money recovered at airport security checkpoints
funding its activities in support of USO airport centers
Document 3: Aviation Security Stakeholder Participation Act of 2012
establish in the TSA an Aviation Security Advisory Committee
Document 4: conduct a terrorism threat assessment of the transportation of chemical, biological, nuclear, and radiological materials
disseminate such assessment to federal partners
Document 5: ensure that all applicants for law enforcement positions with U.S. Customs and Border Protection receive polygraph examinations
periodic background reinvestigations for all of its law enforcement personnel
Document 6: provide payment to first responders for costs associated with providing emergency services at U.S. international borders
costs are not otherwise reimbursed through any federal program
Document 7: sets forth standards and procedures for Department of Homeland Security border searches or seizures of electronic equipment
prohibits a DHS official from considering race, ethnicity, national origin, or religion
Document 8: Defending Quality Postal Delivery for the Future Act
prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from closing or consolidating any post office
Document 9: require the Secretary of Homeland Security to construct two layers of reinforced fencing along the entire international land border
provide for the installation of additional physical barriers, roads, lighting, cameras, and sensors
Document 10: Weapons of Mass Destruction Prevention and Preparedness Act of 2010
direct the Secretaries of Health and Human Services and Agriculture to designate as 'Tier I agents' those agents and toxins

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and acts related to the regulation and safety of food, drugs, and dietary supplements in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to increase civil monetary penalties for introducing into interstate commerce food that is adulterated.
Authorizes additional appropriations for the examination by the Secretary of Health and Human Services of processed food and toothpaste which is being imported.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to require that specified slaughterhouses, processing establishments, and grinding facilities perform specified tests for the presence of Shiga toxin-producing E. coli in beef.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to make grants to assist certain slaughterhouses, processing establishments, and grinding facilities in complying with such requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for research on the development of tests and sampling methodologies for use on imported food.
Requires the Secretary to establish a program under which persons importing food voluntarily agree to abide by specified food and security guidelines.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit direct-to-consumer advertising of a drug in the first three years after the drug's approval.
The Food and Drug Administration may waive the third year of this prohibition for a drug if direct-to-consumer advertising of the drug would have an affirmative value to public health.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish premarket notification requirements for a bioengineered organism intended for a food use or application.

Directs the developer of a bioengineered organism to submit a premarket biotechnology notification to the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) at least 210 days before the organism is first introduced into interstate commerce for a food use or application.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to extend for FY2014-FY2018 the authority of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to collect generic animal drug user fees.
Establishes the total amount of revenue each type of generic user fee shall generate.
Document 7: Authorizes and makes appropriations to carry out the Dietary Supplement Health and Education Act of 1994 (DSHEA) and all applicable regulatory requirements for dietary supplements under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.
Requires the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to fully and appropriately use such funds to regulate dietary supplements.
Document 8: Amends the Public Health Service Act to allow a person to submit an application for licensure of a biological product based on its similarity to a licensed biological product.
Prohibits a biological product from being evaluated against more than one reference product.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize provisional approval of fast track products determined by the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to be adequately safe.
Defines the term 'adequately safe' to mean that the risk of death or morbidity caused directly by an adverse effect of the drug is unlikely to be greater than the combined direct and secondary risks.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to conditionally approve certain cellular therapeutic products without initiation of large-scale clinical trials.
The sponsor of a conditionally approved cellular therapy must apply for approval of the product as a biological product within five years.

topic.name: Animal Welfare and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving the welfare and safety of animals, particularly in breeding and retail contexts.

Document 4: Defines a 'high volume retail breeder' and sets requirements for exercise of dogs.
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to promulgate requirements for the exercise of dogs.
Document 5: Defines 'retail pet store' and outlines exemptions from licensing requirements.
Mandates exercise requirements for dogs held by dealers.
Document 9: Repeats definitions and requirements for 'retail pet store' and exercise periods for dogs.
Establishes conditions under which retail pet stores are exempt from licensing.
Document 10: Amends the Animal Welfare Act regarding sources of dogs and cats for research facilities.
Establishes monetary penalties for violations of the new requirements.

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and opportunities for small businesses through various programs and procurement policies.

Document 1: Scale-Up Manufacturing Investment Company Act of 2015
support debt and equity investments in qualifying manufacturing projects
Document 2: Small Business Women's Procurement Program Improvement Act
contracting opportunities for women-owned small businesses
Document 3: Supporting Entrepreneurial Economic Development Act
foster the startup of businesses or accelerate the growth of fledgling companies
Document 4: Small Business Disaster Recovery Assistance Improvement Act
provide small businesses with immediate disaster assistance
Document 5: study on the participation of small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
evaluating whether federal agencies have met the government-wide goals
Document 6: Job Creation Through Entrepreneurship Act of 2009
provide entrepreneurial training and counseling to veterans
Document 7: amend the Small Business Act to exclude from the definition of 'Indian tribe'
socially or economically disadvantaged shall be determined by the SBA Administrator
Document 8: Expanding Opportunities for Main Street Act
requires a federal agency to award to a small business concern each acquisition contract
Document 9: Cheating Doesn't Pay Act
prohibits the use of disadvantaged business enterprise program funds by any enterprise no longer eligible
Document 10: Maximizing Small Business Competition Act of 2016
authorizes a procurement center representative to review any solicitation for a contract

topic.name: Housing and Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving housing policies, consumer protection in credit and lending, and addressing issues related to low-income housing and homelessness.

Document 1: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
require local governments or other HUD-approved entities to make inspections
Document 2: Amends the Truth In Lending Act
limit the annual percentage rate (APR) applicable to any consumer credit transaction
Document 3: Revises federal bankruptcy law
limits exemption from discharge in bankruptcy for certain educational loans
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
qualify low-income building units that provide housing for full-time students who were homeless youth
Document 5: Amends the National Housing Act
revises the single insurance premium payment for a mortgage
Document 6: Amends the National Housing Act
prohibit Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac from purchasing MERS mortgages
Document 7: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
establishes a cardholder right to repay all existing balances
Document 8: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act
requires written agreement to itemize all fees the originator may charge
Document 9: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
revises the exclusion from the meaning of 'mortgage originator'
Document 10: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937
require public housing agencies to terminate the tenancy of families with income exceeding 125% of the area median income

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conveyance, and disposal of federal lands and properties.

Document 1: removal of deed restrictions related to a parcel of land
described in a specified quitclaim deed from the United States to Accomack County, Virginia
Document 2: requires federal agency real property plans
dispose of excess properties
Document 3: amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act
extends eligibility for allotments to Alaska Native Vietnam veterans
Document 4: requires proceeds from sales to be used for habitat conservation
extends purchase authority with respect to certain areas
Document 5: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey federal land
authorizes mineral exploration activities
Document 6: provides for the conveyance of specified federal land
requires the Secretary of the Interior to reserve a perpetual conservation easement
Document 7: withdraws public lands for military training purposes
prohibits restrictions on mining
Document 8: requires conveyance of land to a partnership
subject to existing rights of federal oil and gas leases
Document 9: authorizes leasing of underutilized federal properties
develop facilities for federal agencies
Document 10: directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey land for redevelopment
requires fair market value determination for federal land
